original_registry_entry.exact_document_title: British Society of Gastroenterology guidelines for the management of iron deficiency anaemia in adults
original_registry_entry.jurisdiction: United Kingdom; international gastroenterology evidence requiring UAE adaptation
original_registry_entry.population: Adults with confirmed or suspected iron deficiency anaemia; pregnancy and paediatric populations require separate evidence.
original_registry_entry.clinical_setting: Primary, gastroenterology, haematology, and investigation-result review.
original_registry_entry.applicability_note: Supports symptom, bleeding, diet, medicine, donation, surgery, family, laboratory, coeliac, upper/lower GI investigation, and follow-up documentation without diagnosing cause or generating treatment.

exact_sections.heading: Initial assessment and risk context
exact_sections.locator: recommendations and sections on history and initial assessment
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports clinician review of iron indices, anaemia, GI symptoms, bleeding, diet, medicines, blood donation, surgery, family history, and comorbidity.

exact_sections.heading: GI investigation, response, and follow-up
exact_sections.locator: recommendations on GI investigation and follow-up
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports recording completed coeliac testing, endoscopy, imaging, response, recurrence, and clinician-decided follow-up without ordering tests.

title: British Society of Gastroenterology guidelines for the management of iron deficiency anaemia in adults
jurisdiction: United Kingdom; international gastroenterology evidence requiring UAE adaptation
population: Adults with confirmed or suspected iron deficiency anaemia; pregnancy and paediatric populations require separate evidence.
intended_setting: Primary, gastroenterology, haematology, and investigation-result review.
access_limitations: One or more PDF tables require structured extraction
Algorithm or figure references preserved as locators; diagram structure not interpreted
